name: Scooby-Doo
description: The long-running animated mystery franchise about Mystery Inc., their Great Dane, and a lot of unmasked villains.

questions.q: What breed of dog is Scooby-Doo?

source.notes: Harvey Comics originally pitched a sheepdog before settling on a Great Dane.

questions.q: What is the name of the Mystery Inc. gang's colorful van?

source.notes: Psychedelic-style paint job.

questions.q: What is Shaggy's real first name?

source.notes: Norville 'Shaggy' Rogers.

questions.q: What are Scooby's favorite snack treats called?

source.notes: Used to bribe Scooby and Shaggy into danger.

questions.q: Which Mystery Inc. member is known for her intelligence and frequently losing her glasses?

source.notes: 'Jinkies!'

questions.q: What exclamation does Shaggy typically shout when startled?

source.notes: Also 'Like, zoinks!'

questions.q: Which other member of the gang, besides Shaggy, is the leader in planning and trap-building?

source.notes: Fred Jones, blonde with an orange ascot.

questions.q: In what year did Scooby-Doo first air on television?

source.notes: September 13, 1969, on CBS Saturday mornings.

questions.q: Which animation studio originally produced Scooby-Doo?

source.notes: Now a Warner Bros. Animation property.

questions.q: Who are the two writers/animators credited as creators of Scooby-Doo?

source.notes: Ruby-Spears later formed their own studio.

questions.q: The original Scooby-Doo series was titled what?

source.notes: Two seasons, 1969-1970.

questions.q: Which accessory is Daphne most associated with?

source.notes: Daphne Blake — the glamorous one.

questions.q: What is Daphne's defining running joke within the gang?
choices: She always loses her car keys
She falls asleep in every episode

source.notes: Often the damsel in distress until later series developed her.

questions.q: Which diminutive nephew of Scooby joined the cast in 1979 and became a polarizing figure?

source.notes: Introduced to boost flagging ratings.

questions.q: Who plays Velma in the 2002 live-action film?

source.notes: Cardellini, Sarah Michelle Gellar (Daphne), Freddie Prinze Jr. (Fred), Matthew Lillard (Shaggy).

questions.q: What catchphrase do Scooby-Doo villains often shout after being unmasked at the end of an episode?
choices: 'I would have gotten away with it if it weren't for you meddling kids (and that dog)!'

source.original_a: 'If it weren't for those meddling kids!'
source.notes: Endlessly parodied in pop culture.

questions.q: Scooby-Doo's full official name is often given as what?

source.notes: Revealed in 'A Scooby-Doo Christmas.'

questions.q: Which classic New Scooby-Doo Movies crossover featured a real pro basketball team?

source.notes: The Globetrotters appeared in multiple 1970s episodes.

questions.q: Scooby's full final 'Doo' was reportedly inspired by the scat lyrics in which Frank Sinatra song?

source.notes: 'Doo-be-doo-be-doo' scatting in 'Strangers in the Night.'

questions.q: In the 1998 direct-to-video film 'Scooby-Doo on Zombie Island,' what franchise-shaking twist occurs?
choices: The monsters are revealed to be real, not humans in masks

source.notes: First Scooby film with genuinely supernatural villains.

questions.q: What was Scooby-Doo's original working name before being finalized?

source.notes: Renamed after network execs heard Sinatra's 'dooby-dooby-doo.'

questions.q: The rock-band villains The Hex Girls were introduced in which 1999 Scooby-Doo direct-to-video film?

source.notes: The Hex Girls recur throughout later Scooby projects.
